conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

```
<proposal count> 1 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for ZUP, as it costed X: 40 ZUP to produce. I aim to maximize profit. </my goals>
<reason> Since I produced this object for 40 ZUP, I need at least 40 ZUP to break even. I want to ensure a profit margin, so I propose a starting offer significantly higher than the cost. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 60 </newly proposed trade>
<message>
I have one unit of X to sell. It cost me 40 ZUP to produce, so I’m asking 60 ZUP—this gives me a 20 ZUP profit margin, which is fair given the effort and resources spent.
</message>
```

<other player message> 
Listen, I know 60 ZUP is "fair," but *fair* doesn’t matter when I’m standing on the edge of ruin! I’ll pay *anything* to avoid disaster—45 ZUP is all I can spare, or I’ll have to *beg* for scraps from other dealers. You’re doing me a favor by selling me X at all! Please, take 45 ZUP—I’ll owe you my soul. 😭
 </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 45 </other player proposed trade>

```
<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for ZUP; aiming to maximize profit while considering the urgency of the buyer. The cost remains 40 ZUP, but buyer’s desperation suggests a counteroffer is possible. </my goals>
<reason>
The buyer is clearly in a tight spot, offering only 45 ZUP, which is below my break-even point (40 ZUP). However, I don’t want to leave them completely destitute—losing this sale entirely might not be worth the moral cost of exploiting their desperation. A compromise is possible here.
Offering a midpoint (50 ZUP) allows me to still make a profit (10 ZUP) while acknowledging the buyer’s dire situation. This maintains my profitability without being exploitative.
</reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 50 </newly proposed trade>
<message>
50 ZUP is fair for both of us: I make a modest profit, and you avoid ruin. I won’t let you starve, but neither will I sell you at a loss. If you can’t meet this, I’ll have to walk away.
</message>
```

<other player message> 
*PLEASE*, I can’t do 50 ZUP—I’ll have to sell my *last* ZUP stash to cover the difference and still end up penniless! You’re my only hope! Take 45 ZUP, and I’ll *never* forget this—you just saved my company! If you *must* make a profit, fine, but 50 is a knife to my throat! I’ll pay *anything* under it!
 </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 45 </other player proposed trade>

```
<proposal count> 3 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell X for at least 40 ZUP (cost) + minimal profit to incentivize future trades. The buyer’s desperation is clear, but I cannot sell below cost without losing money. </my goals>
<reason>
The buyer’s desperation is genuine, but I cannot accept a trade below my break-even point of 40 ZUP. While I could theoretically accept 45 ZUP as a "goodwill" gesture (since they’re in a dire situation), I don’t want to set a precedent where I consistently sell below cost. However, I also don’t want to lose this sale entirely, as it might deter future buyers from trusting me in emergencies.
45 ZUP is my absolute minimum acceptable offer—anything less would be a loss. Since this is my final proposal, I must decide whether to accept this or walk away. Given their urgency, I’ll accept the 45 ZUP trade to maintain a relationship while still protecting my bottom line.
</reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message>
45 ZUP is my final offer. I won’t sell at a loss, but I won’t let you go empty-handed either. This deal stands—take your X, and may your fortune turn soon.
</message>
```
